notes: Educational summaries. Simplified; phrased as 'commonly involved in'.
disclaimer: Educational summary (simplified). Not medical advice; not clinical diagnosis.

Amygdala_L.title: Amygdala (Left)
Amygdala_L.summary: Helps detect biologically relevant salience, especially threat and uncertainty. Supports fear learning and can modulate memory systems so emotionally important events are remembered more strongly. Often engaged during threat-related stimuli and emotional faces tasks.

Amygdala_R.title: Amygdala (Right)
Amygdala_R.summary: Helps detect biologically relevant salience, especially threat and uncertainty. Supports fear learning and can modulate memory systems so emotionally important events are remembered more strongly. Often engaged during threat-related stimuli and emotional faces tasks.

Angular_L.title: Angular gyrus (Left)
Angular_L.summary: Parietal association cortex commonly involved in semantic processing, number-related operations, and multimodal integration during language and attention tasks.

Angular_R.title: Angular gyrus (Right)
Angular_R.summary: Parietal association cortex commonly involved in semantic processing, number-related operations, and multimodal integration during language and attention tasks.

Calcarine_L.title: Calcarine cortex (Left) — primary visual cortex (V1)
Calcarine_L.summary: Primary visual cortex involved in early processing of visual input. It contains a retinotopic map (neighboring neurons represent neighboring points in visual space). Often strongly activated by simple visual stimuli like flashing checkerboards.

Calcarine_R.title: Calcarine cortex (Right) — primary visual cortex (V1)
Calcarine_R.summary: Primary visual cortex involved in early processing of visual input. It contains a retinotopic map (neighboring neurons represent neighboring points in visual space). Often strongly activated by simple visual stimuli like flashing checkerboards.

Caudate_L.title: Caudate nucleus (Left)
Caudate_L.summary: Dorsal striatal region commonly involved in action selection, reinforcement learning, and cognitive control loops with frontal cortex.

Caudate_R.title: Caudate nucleus (Right)
Caudate_R.summary: Dorsal striatal region commonly involved in action selection, reinforcement learning, and cognitive control loops with frontal cortex.

Cerebelum_10_L.title: Cerebellum lobule X (Left)
Cerebelum_10_L.summary: Flocculonodular cerebellar region commonly involved in balance, vestibular processing, and eye-movement stabilization.

Cerebelum_10_R.title: Cerebellum lobule X (Right)
Cerebelum_10_R.summary: Flocculonodular cerebellar region commonly involved in balance, vestibular processing, and eye-movement stabilization.

Cerebelum_3_L.title: Cerebellum lobule III (Left)
Cerebelum_3_L.summary: Anterior cerebellar lobule commonly involved in fine motor coordination and timing of sensorimotor output.

Cerebelum_3_R.title: Cerebellum lobule III (Right)
Cerebelum_3_R.summary: Anterior cerebellar lobule commonly involved in fine motor coordination and timing of sensorimotor output.

Cerebelum_4_5_L.title: Cerebellum lobules IV-V (Left)
Cerebelum_4_5_L.summary: Anterior cerebellar lobules commonly involved in motor execution support, proprioceptive integration, and movement calibration.

Cerebelum_4_5_R.title: Cerebellum lobules IV-V (Right)
Cerebelum_4_5_R.summary: Anterior cerebellar lobules commonly involved in motor execution support, proprioceptive integration, and movement calibration.

Cerebelum_6_L.title: Cerebellum lobule VI (Left)
Cerebelum_6_L.summary: Cerebellar lobule commonly involved in both motor coordination and cognitive timing/sequence support depending on task demands.

Cerebelum_6_R.title: Cerebellum lobule VI (Right)
Cerebelum_6_R.summary: Cerebellar lobule commonly involved in both motor coordination and cognitive timing/sequence support depending on task demands.

Cerebelum_7b_L.title: Cerebellum lobule VIIb (Left)
Cerebelum_7b_L.summary: Posterior cerebellar lobule commonly involved in higher-order cognitive integration and attention-executive coupling.

Cerebelum_7b_R.title: Cerebellum lobule VIIb (Right)
Cerebelum_7b_R.summary: Posterior cerebellar lobule commonly involved in higher-order cognitive integration and attention-executive coupling.

Cerebelum_8_L.title: Cerebellum lobule VIII (Left)
Cerebelum_8_L.summary: Posterior cerebellar lobule commonly involved in sensorimotor timing and adaptation during ongoing movement control.

Cerebelum_8_R.title: Cerebellum lobule VIII (Right)
Cerebelum_8_R.summary: Posterior cerebellar lobule commonly involved in sensorimotor timing and adaptation during ongoing movement control.

Cerebelum_9_L.title: Cerebellum lobule IX (Left)
Cerebelum_9_L.summary: Posterior cerebellar lobule commonly involved in vestibular-autonomic integration and broader internal-state coordination.

Cerebelum_9_R.title: Cerebellum lobule IX (Right)
Cerebelum_9_R.summary: Posterior cerebellar lobule commonly involved in vestibular-autonomic integration and broader internal-state coordination.

Cerebelum_Crus1_L.title: Cerebellum Crus I (Left)
Cerebelum_Crus1_L.summary: Posterolateral cerebellar region commonly involved in language and executive-network coupling with prefrontal association cortex.

Cerebelum_Crus1_R.title: Cerebellum Crus I (Right)
Cerebelum_Crus1_R.summary: Posterolateral cerebellar region commonly involved in language and executive-network coupling with prefrontal association cortex.

Cerebelum_Crus2_L.title: Cerebellum Crus II (Left)
Cerebelum_Crus2_L.summary: Posterolateral cerebellar region commonly involved in high-level association processing, working memory support, and cognitive sequencing.

Cerebelum_Crus2_R.title: Cerebellum Crus II (Right)
Cerebelum_Crus2_R.summary: Posterolateral cerebellar region commonly involved in high-level association processing, working memory support, and cognitive sequencing.

Cingulum_Ant_L.title: Anterior cingulate cortex (Left)
Cingulum_Ant_L.summary: Often involved in attention, conflict monitoring, and error detection. Frequently linked to the affective/motivational component of pain and effortful control. Works with frontal and insular regions during demanding or emotionally salient situations.

Cingulum_Ant_R.title: Anterior cingulate cortex (Right)
Cingulum_Ant_R.summary: Often involved in attention, conflict monitoring, and error detection. Frequently linked to the affective/motivational component of pain and effortful control. Works with frontal and insular regions during demanding or emotionally salient situations.

Cingulum_Mid_L.title: Middle cingulate cortex (Left)
Cingulum_Mid_L.summary: Middle cingulate region commonly involved in response selection, effortful control, and integrating salience with action planning.

Cingulum_Mid_R.title: Middle cingulate cortex (Right)
Cingulum_Mid_R.summary: Middle cingulate region commonly involved in response selection, effortful control, and integrating salience with action planning.

Cingulum_Post_L.title: Posterior cingulate cortex (Left)
Cingulum_Post_L.summary: Posterior cingulate region commonly involved in internally directed cognition, memory context, and default-mode network coordination.

Cingulum_Post_R.title: Posterior cingulate cortex (Right)
Cingulum_Post_R.summary: Posterior cingulate region commonly involved in internally directed cognition, memory context, and default-mode network coordination.

Cuneus_L.title: Cuneus (Left)
Cuneus_L.summary: Medial occipital visual cortex commonly involved in early visual processing, especially central-field and basic scene features.

Cuneus_R.title: Cuneus (Right)
Cuneus_R.summary: Medial occipital visual cortex commonly involved in early visual processing, especially central-field and basic scene features.

Frontal_Inf_Oper_L.title: Inferior frontal gyrus, opercular part (Left)
Frontal_Inf_Oper_L.summary: Inferior frontal opercular cortex commonly involved in language-related sequencing, speech-motor integration, and response inhibition.
aliases: Inferior frontal gyrus, opercular part Left

Frontal_Inf_Oper_R.title: Inferior frontal gyrus, opercular part (Right)
Frontal_Inf_Oper_R.summary: Inferior frontal opercular cortex commonly involved in language-related sequencing, speech-motor integration, and response inhibition.
aliases: Inferior frontal gyrus, opercular part Right

Frontal_Inf_Orb_L.title: Inferior frontal gyrus, orbital part (Left)
Frontal_Inf_Orb_L.summary: Orbitofrontal inferior frontal region commonly involved in valuation, affective appraisal, and context-dependent decision processing.
aliases: Inferior frontal gyrus, orbital part Left

Frontal_Inf_Orb_R.title: Inferior frontal gyrus, orbital part (Right)
Frontal_Inf_Orb_R.summary: Orbitofrontal inferior frontal region commonly involved in valuation, affective appraisal, and context-dependent decision processing.
aliases: Inferior frontal gyrus, orbital part Right

Frontal_Inf_Tri_L.title: Inferior frontal gyrus, triangular part (Left)
Frontal_Inf_Tri_L.summary: Inferior frontal triangular cortex commonly involved in controlled language retrieval, semantic selection, and top-down control of competing representations.
aliases: Inferior frontal gyrus, triangular part Left

Frontal_Inf_Tri_R.title: Inferior frontal gyrus, triangular part (Right)
Frontal_Inf_Tri_R.summary: Inferior frontal triangular cortex commonly involved in controlled language retrieval, semantic selection, and top-down control of competing representations.
aliases: Inferior frontal gyrus, triangular part Right

Frontal_Mid_L.title: Middle frontal gyrus (Left)
Frontal_Mid_L.summary: Dorsolateral prefrontal region commonly involved in working memory, planning, and sustained goal-directed control.

Frontal_Mid_Orb_L.title: Middle frontal gyrus, orbital part (Left)
Frontal_Mid_Orb_L.summary: Orbital middle frontal region commonly involved in outcome evaluation, affective-context integration, and adaptive decision updates.
aliases: Middle frontal gyrus, orbital part Left

Frontal_Mid_Orb_R.title: Middle frontal gyrus, orbital part (Right)
Frontal_Mid_Orb_R.summary: Orbital middle frontal region commonly involved in outcome evaluation, affective-context integration, and adaptive decision updates.
aliases: Middle frontal gyrus, orbital part Right

Frontal_Mid_R.title: Middle frontal gyrus (Right)
Frontal_Mid_R.summary: Dorsolateral prefrontal region commonly involved in working memory, planning, and sustained goal-directed control.

Frontal_Orb_Med_L.title: Medial orbitofrontal / ventromedial prefrontal (Left)
Frontal_Orb_Med_L.summary: Commonly involved in valuation: assessing how rewarding or meaningful something is. Helps integrate emotion, memory, and goals to guide decisions. Often active in reward, preference, and social decision tasks.

Frontal_Orb_Med_R.title: Medial orbitofrontal / ventromedial prefrontal (Right)
Frontal_Orb_Med_R.summary: Commonly involved in valuation: assessing how rewarding or meaningful something is. Helps integrate emotion, memory, and goals to guide decisions. Often active in reward, preference, and social decision tasks.

Frontal_Sup_L.title: Superior frontal gyrus (Left)
Frontal_Sup_L.summary: Superior frontal cortex commonly involved in high-level executive control, self-monitoring, and maintenance of task goals.

Frontal_Sup_Medial_L.title: Superior medial frontal gyrus (Left)
Frontal_Sup_Medial_L.summary: Medial superior frontal region commonly involved in self-referential processing, motivation, and internally guided action selection.

Frontal_Sup_Medial_R.title: Superior medial frontal gyrus (Right)
Frontal_Sup_Medial_R.summary: Medial superior frontal region commonly involved in self-referential processing, motivation, and internally guided action selection.

Frontal_Sup_Orb_L.title: Superior frontal gyrus, orbital part (Left)
Frontal_Sup_Orb_L.summary: Superior orbitofrontal region commonly involved in value-based appraisal, social-emotional context processing, and decision biasing.
aliases: Superior frontal gyrus, orbital part Left

Frontal_Sup_Orb_R.title: Superior frontal gyrus, orbital part (Right)
Frontal_Sup_Orb_R.summary: Superior orbitofrontal region commonly involved in value-based appraisal, social-emotional context processing, and decision biasing.
aliases: Superior frontal gyrus, orbital part Right

Frontal_Sup_R.title: Superior frontal gyrus (Right)
Frontal_Sup_R.summary: Superior frontal cortex commonly involved in high-level executive control, self-monitoring, and maintenance of task goals.

Fusiform_L.title: Fusiform gyrus (Left)
Fusiform_L.summary: Ventral temporal visual-association cortex commonly involved in high-level object and face-related recognition, with task-dependent specialization.

Fusiform_R.title: Fusiform gyrus (Right)
Fusiform_R.summary: Ventral temporal visual-association cortex commonly involved in high-level object and face-related recognition, with task-dependent specialization.

Heschl_L.title: Heschl's gyrus (Left)
Heschl_L.summary: Contains primary auditory cortex. It supports early cortical processing of sound features like frequency and timing, before higher-order areas interpret meaning. Often engaged by tones, speech, and music.

Heschl_R.title: Heschl's gyrus (Right)
Heschl_R.summary: Contains primary auditory cortex. It supports early cortical processing of sound features like frequency and timing, before higher-order areas interpret meaning. Often engaged by tones, speech, and music.

Hippocampus_L.title: Hippocampus (Left)
Hippocampus_L.summary: Supports formation of episodic memories and the binding of context (what happened, where, and when). Important for learning new facts and experiences. Dysfunction is commonly associated with memory impairment.

Hippocampus_R.title: Hippocampus (Right)
Hippocampus_R.summary: Supports formation of episodic memories and the binding of context (what happened, where, and when). Often associated with spatial/contextual aspects of memory, though roles vary. Dysfunction is commonly associated with memory impairment.

Insula_L.title: Insula (Left)
Insula_L.summary: Integrates internal body-state signals ('interoception') with emotional and cognitive context. Commonly involved in pain, nausea/disgust, and awareness of bodily arousal. Often treated as part of a broader salience system that helps prioritize attention.

Insula_R.title: Insula (Right)
Insula_R.summary: Integrates internal body-state signals ('interoception') with emotional and cognitive context. Commonly involved in pain, nausea/disgust, and awareness of bodily arousal. Often treated as part of a broader salience system that helps prioritize attention.

Lingual_L.title: Lingual gyrus (Left)
Lingual_L.summary: Medial occipital-temporal region commonly involved in visual feature analysis, word-form/scene components, and visual memory interactions.

Lingual_R.title: Lingual gyrus (Right)
Lingual_R.summary: Medial occipital-temporal region commonly involved in visual feature analysis, word-form/scene components, and visual memory interactions.

Occipital_Inf_L.title: Inferior occipital gyrus (Left)
Occipital_Inf_L.summary: Inferior occipital visual-association cortex commonly involved in detailed form processing and early stages of object/face pathway analysis.

Occipital_Inf_R.title: Inferior occipital gyrus (Right)
Occipital_Inf_R.summary: Inferior occipital visual-association cortex commonly involved in detailed form processing and early stages of object/face pathway analysis.

Occipital_Mid_L.title: Middle occipital gyrus (Left)
Occipital_Mid_L.summary: Middle occipital cortex commonly involved in intermediate visual feature integration, motion/form interactions, and visuospatial analysis.

Occipital_Mid_R.title: Middle occipital gyrus (Right)
Occipital_Mid_R.summary: Middle occipital cortex commonly involved in intermediate visual feature integration, motion/form interactions, and visuospatial analysis.

Occipital_Sup_L.title: Superior occipital gyrus (Left)
Occipital_Sup_L.summary: Superior occipital cortex commonly involved in visuospatial processing and dorsal visual-stream coordination with parietal attention systems.

Occipital_Sup_R.title: Superior occipital gyrus (Right)
Occipital_Sup_R.summary: Superior occipital cortex commonly involved in visuospatial processing and dorsal visual-stream coordination with parietal attention systems.

Olfactory_L.title: Olfactory cortex (Left)
Olfactory_L.summary: Orbitobasal olfactory region commonly involved in odor-related processing and integration of chemosensory signals with affective valuation.

Olfactory_R.title: Olfactory cortex (Right)
Olfactory_R.summary: Orbitobasal olfactory region commonly involved in odor-related processing and integration of chemosensory signals with affective valuation.

Pallidum_L.title: Globus pallidus (Left)
Pallidum_L.summary: Basal ganglia output nucleus commonly involved in gating motor and cognitive loops via thalamo-cortical regulation.

Pallidum_R.title: Globus pallidus (Right)
Pallidum_R.summary: Basal ganglia output nucleus commonly involved in gating motor and cognitive loops via thalamo-cortical regulation.

Paracentral_Lobule_L.title: Paracentral lobule (Left)
Paracentral_Lobule_L.summary: Medial sensorimotor region commonly involved in lower-limb motor/somatosensory representations and motor set maintenance.

Paracentral_Lobule_R.title: Paracentral lobule (Right)
Paracentral_Lobule_R.summary: Medial sensorimotor region commonly involved in lower-limb motor/somatosensory representations and motor set maintenance.

ParaHippocampal_L.title: Parahippocampal gyrus (Left)
ParaHippocampal_L.summary: Medial temporal region commonly involved in contextual memory encoding, scene representation, and hippocampal memory interface functions.

ParaHippocampal_R.title: Parahippocampal gyrus (Right)
ParaHippocampal_R.summary: Medial temporal region commonly involved in contextual memory encoding, scene representation, and hippocampal memory interface functions.

Parietal_Inf_L.title: Inferior parietal lobule (Left)
Parietal_Inf_L.summary: Inferior parietal association region commonly involved in attention reorienting, multimodal integration, and language-related semantic processing.

Parietal_Inf_R.title: Inferior parietal lobule (Right)
Parietal_Inf_R.summary: Inferior parietal association region commonly involved in attention reorienting, multimodal integration, and language-related semantic processing.

Parietal_Sup_L.title: Superior parietal lobule (Left)
Parietal_Sup_L.summary: Superior parietal region commonly involved in visuospatial attention, sensorimotor integration, and top-down spatial control.

Parietal_Sup_R.title: Superior parietal lobule (Right)
Parietal_Sup_R.summary: Superior parietal region commonly involved in visuospatial attention, sensorimotor integration, and top-down spatial control.

Postcentral_L.title: Postcentral gyrus (Left) — primary somatosensory cortex
Postcentral_L.summary: Primary somatosensory cortex for the body (touch, vibration, proprioception). It maps the opposite (right) side of the body in an orderly 'homunculus-like' organization. Often engaged by tactile stimulation and pain-related input.

Postcentral_R.title: Postcentral gyrus (Right) — primary somatosensory cortex
Postcentral_R.summary: Primary somatosensory cortex for the body (touch, vibration, proprioception). It maps the opposite (left) side of the body in an orderly 'homunculus-like' organization. Often engaged by tactile stimulation and pain-related input.

Precentral_L.title: Precentral gyrus (Left) — primary motor cortex
Precentral_L.summary: Primary motor cortex involved in initiating voluntary movement. It contains a body map and primarily influences the opposite (right) side of the body. Often engaged during finger tapping, speech motor planning, and other motor tasks.

Precentral_R.title: Precentral gyrus (Right) — primary motor cortex
Precentral_R.summary: Primary motor cortex involved in initiating voluntary movement. It contains a body map and primarily influences the opposite (left) side of the body. Often engaged during finger tapping and other motor tasks.

Precuneus_L.title: Precuneus (Left)
Precuneus_L.summary: A medial parietal region often associated with visuospatial imagery and self-referential processing. Frequently discussed as part of the default mode network. Engagement depends strongly on task and mental state.

Precuneus_R.title: Precuneus (Right)
Precuneus_R.summary: A medial parietal region often associated with visuospatial imagery and self-referential processing. Frequently discussed as part of the default mode network. Engagement depends strongly on task and mental state.

Putamen_L.title: Putamen (Left)
Putamen_L.summary: Dorsal striatal region commonly involved in motor program selection, habit-related learning, and reinforcement-driven action tuning.

Putamen_R.title: Putamen (Right)
Putamen_R.summary: Dorsal striatal region commonly involved in motor program selection, habit-related learning, and reinforcement-driven action tuning.

Rectus_L.title: Gyrus rectus (Left)
Rectus_L.summary: Medial orbitofrontal region commonly involved in reward valuation, affective context, and social-emotional appraisal.

Rectus_R.title: Gyrus rectus (Right)
Rectus_R.summary: Medial orbitofrontal region commonly involved in reward valuation, affective context, and social-emotional appraisal.

Rolandic_Oper_L.title: Rolandic operculum (Left)
Rolandic_Oper_L.summary: Peri-sylvian sensorimotor integration region commonly involved in orofacial/speech-motor processing and somatosensory-motor coupling.

Rolandic_Oper_R.title: Rolandic operculum (Right)
Rolandic_Oper_R.summary: Peri-sylvian sensorimotor integration region commonly involved in orofacial/speech-motor processing and somatosensory-motor coupling.

Supp_Motor_Area_L.title: Supplementary motor area (Left)
Supp_Motor_Area_L.summary: Medial frontal motor region commonly involved in movement initiation, motor sequencing, and internally generated action plans.

Supp_Motor_Area_R.title: Supplementary motor area (Right)
Supp_Motor_Area_R.summary: Medial frontal motor region commonly involved in movement initiation, motor sequencing, and internally generated action plans.

SupraMarginal_L.title: Supramarginal gyrus (Left)
SupraMarginal_L.summary: Inferior parietal region commonly involved in phonological processing, sensorimotor integration, and attentional reorienting.

SupraMarginal_R.title: Supramarginal gyrus (Right)
SupraMarginal_R.summary: Inferior parietal region commonly involved in phonological processing, sensorimotor integration, and attentional reorienting.

Temporal_Inf_L.title: Inferior temporal gyrus (Left)
Temporal_Inf_L.summary: High-level ventral temporal cortex commonly involved in complex visual object representations and semantic-visual integration.

Temporal_Inf_R.title: Inferior temporal gyrus (Right)
Temporal_Inf_R.summary: High-level ventral temporal cortex commonly involved in complex visual object representations and semantic-visual integration.

Temporal_Mid_L.title: Middle temporal gyrus (Left)
Temporal_Mid_L.summary: Temporal association cortex commonly involved in semantic processing, multimodal language support, and context-driven integration.

Temporal_Mid_R.title: Middle temporal gyrus (Right)
Temporal_Mid_R.summary: Temporal association cortex commonly involved in semantic processing, multimodal language support, and context-driven integration.

Temporal_Pole_Mid_L.title: Middle temporal pole (Left)
Temporal_Pole_Mid_L.summary: Anterior temporal pole region commonly involved in semantic memory integration and affective-social meaning representation.

Temporal_Pole_Mid_R.title: Middle temporal pole (Right)
Temporal_Pole_Mid_R.summary: Anterior temporal pole region commonly involved in semantic memory integration and affective-social meaning representation.

Temporal_Pole_Sup_L.title: Superior temporal pole (Left)
Temporal_Pole_Sup_L.summary: Anterior superior temporal region commonly involved in socio-emotional and auditory-language context integration.

Temporal_Pole_Sup_R.title: Superior temporal pole (Right)
Temporal_Pole_Sup_R.summary: Anterior superior temporal region commonly involved in socio-emotional and auditory-language context integration.

Temporal_Sup_L.title: Superior temporal gyrus (Left)
Temporal_Sup_L.summary: Auditory association cortex that helps analyze complex sounds, including speech. In many people, the left superior temporal region is strongly involved in language comprehension. It often works with frontal language areas during listening.

Temporal_Sup_R.title: Superior temporal gyrus (Right)
Temporal_Sup_R.summary: Auditory association cortex that helps analyze complex sounds. The right superior temporal region is often associated with prosody and aspects of music perception, though roles vary by task and person. It collaborates with broader attention and memory systems during listening.

Thalamus_L.title: Thalamus (Left)
Thalamus_L.summary: A major relay and gating hub for sensory and motor information on the way to cortex. Helps regulate arousal, attention, and the 'broadcasting' of signals to cortical networks. Often involved broadly across many tasks rather than one narrow function.

Thalamus_R.title: Thalamus (Right)
Thalamus_R.summary: A major relay and gating hub for sensory and motor information on the way to cortex. Helps regulate arousal, attention, and the 'broadcasting' of signals to cortical networks. Often involved broadly across many tasks rather than one narrow function.

Vermis_1_2.title: Vermis lobules I-II
Vermis_1_2.summary: Midline anterior cerebellar vermis region commonly involved in axial motor tone and early sensorimotor coordination.

Vermis_10.title: Vermis lobule X
Vermis_10.summary: Flocculonodular midline vermis region commonly involved in balance control, vestibular signaling, and ocular stabilization.

Vermis_3.title: Vermis lobule III
Vermis_3.summary: Midline cerebellar vermis region commonly involved in postural adjustment and trunk-focused motor coordination.

Vermis_4_5.title: Vermis lobules IV-V
Vermis_4_5.summary: Midline cerebellar vermis region commonly involved in gait/posture control and integration of proprioceptive motor feedback.

Vermis_6.title: Vermis lobule VI
Vermis_6.summary: Midline cerebellar vermis region commonly involved in sensorimotor coordination with increasing cognitive-affective coupling.

Vermis_7.title: Vermis lobule VII
Vermis_7.summary: Midline posterior vermis region commonly involved in arousal, affective modulation, and cognitive-autonomic coordination.

Vermis_8.title: Vermis lobule VIII
Vermis_8.summary: Midline posterior vermis region commonly involved in ongoing motor timing and postural dynamics during movement.

Vermis_9.title: Vermis lobule IX
Vermis_9.summary: Inferior posterior vermis region commonly involved in vestibular and autonomic-state integration with internal regulation.
